results.name: both catalog assets' κ pins are REAL — on-disk bytes re-derive to their declared sha256 (L5, oracle)
tile A streams + verifies from origin (4K decodes)
DEDUP-INSTANT — tile B (same κ) materializes from the κ-store with ZERO origin bytes (O(1), Law L1)
a DIFFERENT κ (tile C) streams fresh from origin — no false dedup
SHOW-ME-THE-κ — the panel re-derives the resident bytes to the declared κ (L5)
OFFLINE-EVERYTHING — origin cut, a streamed tile still plays from the κ-store (L3 + serverless)

spec: Holo Hub Phase 2a: a real catalog of live κ-streaming tiles. Each tile is a real κ-object verified to its declared κ before decode and persisted in the OpfsKappaStore. Witnessed live: stream+verify (4K), DEDUP-INSTANT (shared κ ⇒ zero origin bytes, O(1)), distinct κ streams fresh, show-me-the-κ re-derivation, and offline-everything (origin cut ⇒ still plays from the κ-store).
